channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Arc Raiders tops 16 million sales and could be heading to China
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

May 18, 2026

2 Min Read

Image via Nexon / Embark Studios

Arc Raiders has sold over 16 million units worldwide, according to publisher Nexon. The extraction shooter—developed by Embark Studios—sold an additional 4.6 million units in Q1 to achieve that milestone.

The South Korean-Japanese company revealed those figures in its fiscal report for the first quarter (Q1) ended March 31, 2026. 

Nexon said more than half of active Arc Raiders players spent over 100 hours in the title during Q1, resulting in 1.5 billion hours of playtime.

The publisher claimed that ongoing success demonstrates its ability to build global franchises at scale, and described Arc Raiders as the "most successful release in company history."

Notably, Nexon said it has obtained an ISBN license in China and has scheduled multiple closed beta tests for Arc Raiders in the lucrative but highly regulated market. For context, it is currently illegal to operate or monetise a video game in China without an ISBN license.

Related:The Nex Playground is launching in the UK and Ireland

Thanks to contributions from Arc Raiders and other titles such as MapleStory, operating income reached quarterly record highs during Q1. 

Q1 revenue increased by 34 percent year-over-year to 152.2 billion yen ($957.6 million), while operating income rose by 40 percent to 58.2 billion yen ($366.2 million) over the same period. 

Net income exceeded Nexon's outlook after increasing by 118 percent year-on-year to 57.2 billion yen ($360 million).

The company made headway with its recently announced transformation initiative by cancelling three projects and allocating funding to two other promising titles: Nakwon: Last Paradise and Woochi the Wayfarer.

"Creative talent has been redeployed to projects that can capture and sustain a global audience," added the company. "A renewed focus on cost management yielded a decision to maintain flat HR costs and headcount in 2026."

Development of the Chinese version of Dungeon&Fighter Mobile has also been transferred to Tencent to enable "greater hyperlocalization and player engagement." 

Nexon's internal Neople Studio will retain creative control over the co-development with Tencent. 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/arc-raiders-tops-16-million-sales-and-could-be-heading-to-china
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: The Nex Playground is launching in the UK and Ireland
item.content:encoded:
Chris Kerr,

Senior Editor, News,GameDeveloper.com

May 18, 2026

2 Min Read

Nex Playground, the motion-tracking video game console that outsold Xbox in the United States during the 2025 holiday season (via The Verge), is launching in the United Kingdom and Republic of Ireland. 

The move marks the first time the console, pitched as a controller-free "active play system" designed to get families moving together, is being made available outside of North America.

Nex president and head of international Tom Kang said the company is taking a big swing in 2026 by attempting to establish the platform on the global stage.

The Nex Playground is currently on track to surpass 1 million sales in North America and is now available for pre-order at major UK retailers including Argos, Smyths, and Amazon. 

It will retail for £269 (€319) in the region and come with five free starter games so people can play straight out of the box. The product is expected to hit shelves starting in late June.

Related:Subnautica 2 has surpassed 2 million sales in 12 hours

Additional software is accessible via a quarterly or annual 'Play Pass' subscription that features over 60 titles, including some based on major franchises like Bluey, Avatar: The Last Airbender, and Dora the Explorer. 

Annual and quarterly Play Pass subscriptions will cost £90 (€99) and £45 (€49), respectively. 

Given the device is targeting younger players, Nex has stressed that trust and safety are being treated as a "core part" of the platform rather than a mandatory compliance layer. 

"Motion-tracking data and processing stay local to the device, and every system includes a built-in camera cover," read a press release.

"As the sole publisher of every game on the platform, Nex is directly accountable for the trust, safety, and certification of the entire experience. Nex Playground aligns with GDPR requirements, earning PEGI 3 age ratings on the included starter bundle, and maintaining kidSAFE+ certification alongside COPPA compliance in the United States."

The Nex Playground has also been designed as a closed ecosystem with no advertisements or mature content. Nex said this will create a "secure gaming environment" for children. 

About the Author

Senior Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Game Developer news editor Chris Kerr is an award-winning reporter with over a decade of experience in the game industry. His byline has appeared in notable print and digital publications including Edge, Stuff, Wireframe, International Business Times, and PocketGamer.biz. Throughout his career, Chris has covered major industry events including GDC, PAX Australia, Gamescom, Paris Games Week, and Develop Brighton. 



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/the-nex-playground-is-launching-in-the-uk-and-ireland
